reasoning:
The question asks if Gukesh D's Elo rating on Wikipedia will increase by at least 1% (approx. 27.9 points from a base of ~2794) across three resolution dates relative to the base date of October 26, 2025 (actually referencing late 2024 based on when Gukesh reached 2794). For an elite chess player rated near 2800, a rating increase of 28 points in a short period is extraordinarily difficult, requiring a performance of nearly 2.8 points above expected score (since K=10 for players above 2400). Scoring almost 3 points above expected in top-level events (like a World Championship match) requires a historically unprecedented sweep.
For the first two dates (Nov 2 and Nov 25), the FIDE November rating list will likely be used, severely restricting the time available to accumulate such a large rating increase. Furthermore, Wikipedia's FIDE rankings largely update only monthly, meaning the Nov 25 value will likely simply mirror the Nov 1 value. For the final date (January 24), although there is marginally more time for additional events like the World Championship or Tata Steel, gaining 28 points to breach 2820+ remains exceedingly rare (a feat achieved by very few players in history). The probabilities are thus assigned very low values, increasing slightly as the time horizon extends to account for increased uncertainty and the small possibility of dramatic, sustained over-performance.
